title: Ancient Computer Discovered in Shipwreck
story: Imagine finding a 2,000-year-old computer underwater! That's what happened when Greek archaeologist Valerios Stais made an incredible discovery on May 17, 1902. While exploring an ancient shipwreck near the island of Antikythera, he found a mysterious device with gears and dials. This rusty contraption turned out to be the Antikythera mechanism, an ancient mechanical analog computer used to predict astronomical positions and eclipses. It's like finding an iPhone from ancient Greece!
activity_question: What type of device did Valerios Stais discover?
follow_up_question: What was the Antikythera mechanism used to predict?

title: Birth of a Digital Giant

activity_question: What was being built in London in 1899?
bonus_fact: The museum is often called the V&A for short.

title: Marathon Baseball: Cubs vs. Braves
story: Batter up! In 1927, baseball fans witnessed an epic showdown. The Chicago Cubs and Boston Braves locked horns in a game that seemed endless. At Braves Field in Boston, the teams battled for 22 grueling innings on May 17. Finally, the Cubs emerged victorious with a nail-biting 4-3 win. It was a true test of endurance for players and spectators alike!
activity_question: Which team won the marathon baseball game?
follow_up_question: How many innings did this unusually long game last?

title: Allies Make Surprise Landing in New Guinea
story: Splash! Allied forces hit the beaches of Dutch New Guinea on May 17, 1944. Operation Straightline caught the enemy off guard. Soldiers and supplies poured onto the shore, securing a vital foothold. This daring move helped the Allies push back enemy forces in the Pacific during World War II.
activity_question: What was the name of the Allied operation in Dutch New Guinea?
bonus_fact: New Guinea is the world's second-largest island.

title: A New Name for Ireland
story: Handshakes and smiles all around! The British government officially recognized the Republic of Ireland in 1949. This May 17 marked a big change from the old name, Irish Free State. It was a friendly step between neighbors, showing how countries can grow and change over time.
activity_question: What was Ireland's previous name before becoming the Republic of Ireland?
follow_up_question: Can you think of other countries that have changed their names?
